AI Palestra.AI Platform

Demo brief
How it works1 · Primary2 · Secondary3 · University4 · IndustryAbout

Methodology

How Palestra AI works

Generative AI as a medium for human formation — not a substitute for it. Two ideas carry the whole architecture: the Teenager Circle (near-peer instruction) and the Four Stations (Talento → Valore → Successo → Comic-Strip Review).

The methodological core

The Teenager Circle

Secondary-school students (15–18) are placed in the role of primary educators — not assistants, not facilitators — of the AI-literacy moments for primary-school pupils (8–11). They design the station exercises, lead the discussion, manage the interaction with the generative model, and debrief the younger pupils.

The mechanism rests on two findings from learning science: near-peer instruction outperforms far-distance instruction for concept anchoring, and the act of teaching consolidates the teacher's own learning more durably than passive exposure.

In Italy, this runs through the school-to-work programme (PCTO / Formazione Scuola Lavoro): the Liceo Majorana cohort serves through PCTO, while primary pupils are the audience they engage with.

Dual-beneficiary model

Primary pupils (8–11)

Beneficiaries of AI literacy, in the strict sense.

Liceo students (15–18)

Beneficiaries of a transformative PCTO experience — a measurable shift from consumers, to designers, to "AI ambassadors": students who hold themselves accountable for how AI is introduced to those younger than them.

Across the two cohorts (~80 + ~70 students), this transformation is now recognised as a primary outcome of the programme in its own right.

The operational unit

The Four Stations

Each station is a self-contained 25–35 minute experience, designed and led by Liceo Majorana co-educators, with one teacher observing. Stations 1–3 build the human foundations — who I am, what I can contribute, how I read success. Station 4 then introduces generative AI as something to be inspected through those foundations: the pupil checks the AI, not the other way around.

1 71%

Talento

Talent & self-discovery

Each pupil discovers what they are good at, what excites them, what is theirs. Talent is plural — every pupil has their own, and discovering it precedes any technical literacy.

Top concept recall (POST, 19 May 2026)

"Ognuno ha talenti diversi" — everyone has different talents

positive station rating

2 72%

Valore

From talent to contribution

Personal talent acquires meaning when it contributes to others. The pupil moves from "what am I good at" to "my talent has value when it helps someone else".

Top concept recall (POST, 19 May 2026)

"Il mio talento può aiutare gli altri" — my talent can help others

positive station rating

3 78%

Successo

Redefining success

Success is not synonymous with winning. It includes effort, perseverance, and learning from failure — gently challenging the default school definition of success.

Top concept recall (POST, 19 May 2026)

"Il successo non è solo vincere" — success is not only about winning

positive rating — the most liked of the three concept stations

61% of pupils named this as the lesson they remembered — the single largest concept-recall figure of the entire POST evaluation.

4 64%

Comic-Strip Review

Human-in-the-Loop

Pupils inspect real AI-generated comics next to the source story and flag what is wrong, missing, or stereotyped. The pupil checks the AI, not the other way around.

Top concept recall (POST, 19 May 2026)

"L'IA può sbagliare i disegni" — AI can get the drawings wrong

rated the station "tantissimo" — the most liked station of the entire programme

1 pupil in 3 independently formulated "AI can be wrong, I need to check" — what we call Human-in-the-Loop a 10 anni.

Rotation: the four stations run in parallel; each class rotates through all four during a single half-day session. Each station is led by two Liceo Majorana students in tandem. The primary teacher observes and takes the Diario di Bordo Docenti notes — a primary qualitative data source.

See Level 1 — Primary School in detail

Where research meets practice

Open questions for Phase II

The Teenager Circle is simultaneously the educational mechanism and the measurement instrument — the Liceo Majorana 5A co-educators conducted the 1-to-1 POST interviews with the primary pupils themselves. This is a deliberate design choice, but it raises questions that call for academic collaboration:

  • Is the entanglement of mechanism and measurement instrument an interesting design feature, or a confound to engineer out?
  • What evaluation instruments capture the transition from AI users to "AI ambassadors" without collapsing the construct into reductive proxies?
  • What governance and ethical safeguards are required for near-peer AI instruction at scale, when minors are on both sides of the educator/learner relationship?
  • Which dimensions of the Teenager Circle are robust across cultural and institutional contexts, and which are specific to the Italian educational fabric in which they emerged?
AI Palestra.AI Platform

Educational architecture for human–AI co-formation. Jointly developed with the schools and partners of the Palestra AI pilot.
